items.q: What is the technical definition of a recession?
items.correct: Two consecutive quarters of negative real GDP growth — a contraction of the economy.
items.misconception: A recession is when prices fall and people lose jobs.
items.random_wrong: A recession is when the stock market crashes.
items.why: Job losses and stock falls often accompany recessions but don't define them. The technical definition is GDP-based. Students conflate symptoms (unemployment) with definition (negative growth).

items.q: What is the difference between revenue and profit?
items.correct: Revenue is total income from sales (price x quantity); profit is what remains after deducting costs from revenue.
items.misconception: Revenue and profit mean the same thing.
items.random_wrong: Revenue is profit before tax and profit is revenue after tax.
items.why: A business with high revenue can still make a loss if costs exceed it. Revenue = sales income; profit = revenue minus costs. Conflating them gives nonsensical analysis.

items.correct: The change from one additional unit — marginal cost is the cost of producing one more unit; marginal utility is the satisfaction from one more unit. Marginal is incremental, not total or average.
items.misconception: Marginal cost is the total cost of producing all units.
items.why: 'Marginal' has a specific technical meaning: the next unit. Profit maximisation, optimal consumption and many other results depend on marginal — not total or average — quantities. Students conflate the three measures.

items.q: A rise in oil prices that raises firm costs is:
items.why: Correct answer: Cost-push. “Demand-pull” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Coal miners unemployed after pit closures is mainly:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Structural. “Frictional” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: CPI rose from 120 to 126. Inflation rate (%) =
items.why: Exact answer: 5. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
